content.problemStatement:
Write a program to input an array and sort it in ascending order using the heap sort algorithm.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer nnn, denoting the length of an array.
The second line of input will contain n elements a1,a2,a3,a4....an.


Output Format
In a single-line print, the array in ascending order.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers a1, a2, ..., aN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the array sorted in ascending order on a single line. Separate the numbers by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The single test case contains 7 numbers. After applying heap sort they are ordered as shown.
Heap sort arranges the five numbers in non‑decreasing order.
Sorting a reverse‑sorted array yields the ascending sequence.

solution.approach: We use the classic heap sort algorithm. First we build a max‑heap from the input array in O(N) time by calling heapify from the last non‑leaf node up to the root. Then we repeatedly swap the root (maximum element) with the last element of the current heap, shrink the heap size by one and heapify the new root. This yields the array sorted in ascending order. The algorithm works in‑place, needs only O(1) extra space, and runs in O(N log N) time per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case, O(T * N log N) overall.
